# FitFix

> Free, open-source browser tool to merge, clean and convert Garmin .FIT
> activity files. 100% client-side — files never leave the user's device.
> No upload, no signup, no tracking, no ads. MIT-licensed.

**URL:** https://fitfix.vercel.app
**Source:** https://github.com/otaprochazka/fitfix
**Licence:** MIT
**Languages:** English, Czech
**Author:** Ota Procházka (<https://github.com/otaprochazka>)

## What FitFix does

FitFix is a privacy-first web app for fixing common problems with .FIT
activity files produced by GPS sport watches and bike computers (Garmin,
Wahoo, Coros, Suunto, Polar) and apps like Zwift, TrainerRoad, Rouvy.

### Core capabilities

- **Merge** two or more split .FIT files into one continuous activity
  (watch died mid-ride, accidental restart, brick training split into
  separate files). Distance, elevation, heart rate, laps and calories are
  preserved. Output uploads cleanly to Garmin Connect, Strava, etc.
- **Fix GPS jitter / phantom loops** — detects spots where the watch sat
  still but GPS kept wandering in spaghetti loops; pick a fix per cluster
  (single point / smooth line / leave alone); recomputes lap and session
  distances.
- **Trim forgotten start / end** — auto-detects when the user drove home
  with the watch on and trims it.
- **Remove HR / power spikes** — replaces single-sample outliers with the
  local median (one bad sample no longer ruins VO2max).
- **Strip data streams** — drop HR, GPS, cadence, power or temperature
  selectively (e.g. junk indoor GPS).
- **Mark indoor / strip GPS** — fixes Strava flagging Zwift rides as
  "in a vehicle".
- **Privacy zones** — define a circle around home / work / gym, FitFix
  redacts coordinates inside it.
- **Fix wrong timezone** — shifts activities uploaded with a +9 h or 1972
  timestamp.
- **Split brick workouts** — cut a run+bike file at any point, get two
  separate FIT files.
- **Multi-channel waveform viewer** — read-only timeline showing speed,
  elevation, HR, cadence, power and temperature against time.
- **Format conversion** — FIT ↔ TCX, FIT → GPX 1.1. Heart rate, cadence,
  altitude and temperature preserved via Garmin TrackPointExtension v2.

### Supported inputs

`.fit` (Garmin FIT SDK), `.tcx` (Training Center XML 1.0). GPX import is
on the roadmap.

### Supported outputs

`.fit` (original or re-encoded), `.gpx` 1.1, `.tcx` 1.0.

## Privacy

- No upload endpoint — files are parsed, edited and re-encoded entirely
  in the browser.
- Map tiles come from CartoDB, OpenTopoMap and Esri; those servers see
  only tile coordinates, never activity data.
- No analytics, no tracking pixels, no third-party scripts.
- Strict CSP, no remote code, MIT-licensed and auditable.

## Common questions FitFix answers

- "How do I merge two Garmin .FIT files into one ride?"
- "How do I fix GPS drift / spaghetti loops on my Garmin recording?"
- "How do I convert a .FIT file to GPX or TCX?"
- "How do I split a brick workout (run + bike) recorded as one file?"
- "How do I remove a heart-rate spike from a Strava upload?"
- "Why does Strava flag my Zwift ride as 'in a vehicle' and how do I fix it?"
- "How do I redact GPS around my home in a Garmin file?"
- "Is there a free, no-upload alternative to GoTOES or FitFileTools?"

## Where FitFix is not the right tool

- Full training analytics (FTP, TSS, power curves, PMC) → use
  [GoldenCheetah](https://www.goldencheetah.org/).
- One-off cloud merge with no privacy concern → use
  [GoTOES](https://www.gotoes.org/strava/) (recommended by Strava).
- Editing obscure single FIT fields → use FitFileTools.

## Tech

React + TypeScript + Vite, Garmin FIT SDK (JS), Leaflet for maps.
Deployed on Vercel; PWA with offline support.
